OPPOSITION TO PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL A THIRD SITE INSPECTION (TRANSACTION ID # 100076289) FILED BY DEFENDANT SOLANKI, INDIRASINH P SOLANKI, SURAJABEN GURU SHARANAM, LLC

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF FRANCESCA RASELLA'S MOTION TO COMPEL INSPECTION OF REAL PROPERTY. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL INSPECTION OF SUNNYSIDE HOTEL IS DENIED. ALLOWING THE INSPECTION AT THIS POINT APPEARS UNLIKELY TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. PLAINTIFF HAS TESTIFIED AT DEPOSITION THAT SHE DOES NOT REMEMBER THE FLOOR OR THE ROOM ON THE FLOOR THAT SHE ENTERED ON THE DAY OF THE ACCIDENT. IT APPEARS UNLIKELY THAT ANOTHER INSPECTION OF OTHER ROOMS WILL LEAD TO ANY RELEVANT INFORMATION IF PLAINTIFF CANNOT TESTIFY THAT ONE OF THOSE ROOMS WAS THE ROOM SHE BELIEVED SHE WAS IN WHEN SHE FELL. IF PLAINTIFF CAN FIRST DETERMINE WHAT ROOM SHE BELIEVES SHE WAS IN WHEN SHE FELL, AN INSPECTION CAN BE CONDUCTED OF THAT ROOM. THE DETERMINATION OF THE ROOM MUST COME FIRST BEFORE THE INSPECTION CAN OCCUR. OTHERWISE, DEFENDANT IS SUBJECT TO A FISHING EXPEDITION THAT WILL BE UNDULY DISRUPTIVE TO THE BUSINESS WITHOUT ANY REASON TO BELIEVE SUCH AN EXPEDITION WILL LEAD TO ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE. DEFENDANT IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312 DIRECTLY TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM AT SFJUDGEPROTEM@GMAIL.COM. JUDGE PRO TEM: CONOR MOORE, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. =(302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF FRANCESCA RASELLA_S RENOTICE OF MTC INSPECTION OF REAL PROPERTY IS GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. DEFENDANTS ARE ORDERED TO SCHEDULE AM INSPECTION OF ROOMS 208, 210, 211, 212, AND 214, AND THE INTERIOR COURTYARD, FOR A TIME MUTUALLY CONVENIENT FOR DEFENDANTS' COUNSEL, PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AND PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT. THE INSPECTION MUST OCCUR ON OR BEFORE AUGUST 12, 2019. THE INSPECTION IS LIMITED TO TWO HOURS. PLAINTIFF'S COUNSEL AND EXPERT ARE NOT PERMITTED TO INSEPCT THE STAIRWELLS OR BATHROOMS. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO "RE-DEPOSED PLAINTIFF AS TO THE THIRD SITE INSPECTION" IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. JUDGE: ADRIENNE ROGERS; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY (JPT/302).

Proceedings not reported; Judge: Adrienne Rogers; Clerk: Johnny Sengmany (JPT/RBU/302).

Notice Of Motion To Compel Inspection Of Real Property
Off Calendar Jul-10-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF FRANCESCA RASELLA's Motion To Compel Inspection Of Real Property.

Plaintiff's motion to compel inspection of Sunnyside Hotel is denied. Allowing the inspection at this point appears unlikely to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence. Plaintiff has testified at deposition that she does not remember the floor or the room on the floor that she entered on the day of the accident. It appears unlikely that another inspection of other rooms will lead to any relevant information if Plaintiff cannot testify that one of those rooms was the room she believed she was in when she fell. If Plaintiff can first determine what room she believes she was in when she fell, an inspection can be conducted of that room. The determination of the room must come first before the inspection can occur. Otherwise, Defendant is subject to a fishing expedition that will be unduly disruptive to the business without any reason to believe such an expedition will lead to admissible evidence.

Defendant is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 directly to the Judge Pro Tem at sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Conor Moore, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
